common.binary

Class RandomAccessWriter

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream
      • common.binary.AbstractRandomAccessStream
        • common.binary.RandomAccessWriter
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    BinaryOutput, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class RandomAccessWriter
extends AbstractRandomAccessStream
implements BinaryOutput
    Provides a seekable binary stream writer backed by RandomAccessFile.

    Supports in-place file modification and writing primitive data types using a configurable byte order (endianness).

    Since:
    8 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from class common.binary.AbstractRandomAccessStream

        fpath, raf

      • Fields inherited from class common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream

        byteOrder, positionStack

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      RandomAccessWriter(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
      Constructs a random-access writer using ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN by default operating under read-write ("rw") mode.
      RandomAccessWriter(java.nio.file.Path fpath, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Constructs a random-access writer with a specific byte order configuration operating under read-write ("rw") mode.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void writeByte(byte value)
      Writes a single signed byte at the current position.
      void writeBytes(byte[] data)
      Writes an array of raw bytes sequentially at the current position.
      void writeBytes(byte[] data, int length)
      Writes an array of bytes, ensuring the file pointer advances exactly by the specified length.
      void writeDouble(double value)
      Writes a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeFloat(float value)
      Writes a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeInteger(int value)
      Writes a 32-bit signed integer value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeLong(long value)
      Writes a 64-bit signed long value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeNullTerminatedString(java.lang.String s, java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
      Writes a C-style null-terminated string using the specified character set.
      void writeShort(short value)
      Writes a 16-bit short value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeUnsignedInteger(long value)
      Writes an unsigned 32-bit integer value matching the active byte endianness rules.
      void writeUnsignedInteger24(int value)
      Writes an unsigned 24-bit integer value matching the active byte endianness rules.

      • Methods inherited from class common.binary.AbstractRandomAccessStream

        close, getCurrentPosition, getPath, length, seek

      • Methods inherited from class common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream

        checkBounds, getByteOrder, hasRemaining, hasRemaining, mark, remaining, reset, setByteOrder, skip

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      • Methods inherited from interface common.binary.BinaryOutput

        close, getByteOrder, getCurrentPosition, length, mark, reset, seek, setByteOrder

    • Constructor Detail

      • RandomAccessWriter

        public RandomAccessWriter(java.nio.file.Path fpath,
                          java.nio.ByteOrder order)
                   throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a random-access writer with a specific byte order configuration operating under read-write ("rw") mode.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the target file on disk
        order - the byte order used when writing multi-byte values
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while opening the file access channel

      • RandomAccessWriter

        public RandomAccessWriter(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
                   throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a random-access writer using ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN by default operating under read-write ("rw") mode.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the target file on disk
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while opening the file access channel

    • Method Detail

      • writeByte

        public void writeByte(byte value)
               throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a single signed byte at the current position.
        Specified by:
        writeByte in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the byte value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeBytes

        public void writeBytes(byte[] data)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Writes an array of raw bytes sequentially at the current position.
        Specified by:
        writeBytes in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        data - the byte array to write
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if data is null
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeBytes

        public void writeBytes(byte[] data,
                       int length)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Writes an array of bytes, ensuring the file pointer advances exactly by the specified length.

        If the input array is shorter than length, it is padded with trailing zeros (0x00). If the input array is longer than length, only the first length bytes are written into the stream.

        Parameters:
        data - the data payload block to write (may be null, treated as all zeros)
        length - the exact number of bytes to write
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if length is negative
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeShort

        public void writeShort(short value)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a 16-bit short value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Specified by:
        writeShort in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the short integer value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeInteger

        public void writeInteger(int value)
                  throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a 32-bit signed integer value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Specified by:
        writeInteger in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the integer value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeUnsignedInteger

        public void writeUnsignedInteger(long value)
                          throws java.io.IOException
        Writes an unsigned 32-bit integer value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Parameters:
        value - the unsigned integer value represented as a long
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the value falls outside of valid unsigned 32-bit limits
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeUnsignedInteger24

        public void writeUnsignedInteger24(int value)
                            throws java.io.IOException
        Writes an unsigned 24-bit integer value matching the active byte endianness rules. Only the lowest 24 bits of the supplied value are written.
        Parameters:
        value - the integer value containing the 24-bit pattern to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeLong

        public void writeLong(long value)
               throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a 64-bit signed long value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Specified by:
        writeLong in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the long value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeFloat

        public void writeFloat(float value)
                throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Specified by:
        writeFloat in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the float value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeDouble

        public void writeDouble(double value)
                 throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value matching the active byte endianness rules.
        Specified by:
        writeDouble in interface BinaryOutput
        Parameters:
        value - the double value to write
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel

      • writeNullTerminatedString

        public void writeNullTerminatedString(java.lang.String s,
                                      java.nio.charset.Charset charset)
                               throws java.io.IOException
        Writes a C-style null-terminated string using the specified character set.

        A trailing 0x00 byte is always written. If the string is null, only the terminator is written.

        Parameters:
        s - the string to write, or null to write only the terminator
        charset - the character set used to encode the string
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if charset is null
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs on the storage channel
